name: Gothic bridge of San Benito circular from the train station Cañaveral
name_original: Puente gótico de San Benito circular desde la estación de tren Cañaveral
region_intro: Hiking around Cañaveral, located in Extremadura, Spain, offers trails through river valleys, historic sites, and natural park areas. The region features a mix of open countryside and wooded sections, with routes often incorporating views of reservoirs and ancient structures. These trails provide opportunities to explore the local flora and fauna within a varied terrain. Best hiking trails around Cañaveral The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Mirabel – circular through the Umbría del Berbechoso - Monfragüe National Park Biosphere (PR-CC 66), a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Monfragüe National Park Biosphere, offering varied natural scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gothic bridge of San Benito circular from the train station Cañaveral, an easy 4.5 miles (7.2 km) path. This trail features the historic Gothic bridge and follows a circular path from the train station. Local hikers also love the Pondside Campsite loop from Cañaveral, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Hiking around Cañaveral is defined by river valleys, historic bridges, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes. The routes in Cañaveral are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Cañaveral's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cañaveral?
A: There are 8 hiking routes available around Cañaveral, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the region's diverse landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or families in Cañaveral?
A: Yes, Cañaveral offers several easy hiking options suitable for beginners and families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272212849/gothic-bridge-of-san-benito-circular-from-the-train-station-canaveral" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gothic bridge of San Benito circular from the train station Cañaveral&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.5-mile (7.2 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pondside Campsite loop from Cañaveral&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.1-mile (5.0 km) trail, both offering pleasant walks through the local countryside.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Cañaveral?
A: Hikes in Cañaveral vary in duration depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pondside Campsite loop from Cañaveral&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes, while moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990746024/castillo-de-mirabel-circular-through-the-umbria-del-berbechoso-monfraguee-national-park-biosphere-pr-cc-66" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Mirabel circular&lt;/a&gt; might take around 4 hours 36 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes can take over 6 hours.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Cañaveral?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Cañaveral are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990746024/castillo-de-mirabel-circular-through-the-umbria-del-berbechoso-monfraguee-national-park-biosphere-pr-cc-66" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Mirabel circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272212849/gothic-bridge-of-san-benito-circular-from-the-train-station-canaveral" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gothic bridge of San Benito circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Cañaveral?
A: Hiking trails in Cañaveral often feature a mix of natural and historical sights. You can expect views of river valleys, reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3789378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and ancient structures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3796608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Benito Gothic Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable historical landmark along some routes, and you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5676800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Floripes Tower&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to hike in Cañaveral?
A: The best time to hike in Cañaveral, located in Extremadura, Spain, is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any hikes with scenic viewpoints in the Cañaveral area?
A: Yes, the region offers several opportunities for scenic views. Many trails provide vistas over the surrounding countryside, river valleys, and reservoirs. Look for routes that pass by highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3789378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Alcántara Reservoir and Almonte Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; for impressive panoramic sights.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cañaveral?
A: The hiking experience in Cañaveral is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, which includes river valleys and natural park areas, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historic sites like ancient bridges.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cañaveral?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Spain, especially in natural areas. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like Monfragüe National Park, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Check specific park regulations if you plan to enter a national park section.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Cañaveral?
A: Cañaveral is a small town, and you'll find local establishments within the town itself where you can refuel before or after your hike. For trails further afield, it's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, as amenities might be scarce directly along the routes.

Q: What should I wear for spring hiking in Cañaveral?
A: For spring hiking in Cañaveral, layered clothing is recommended as temperatures can vary throughout the day. Opt for breathable, moisture-wicking fabrics. A light jacket or fleece is useful for cooler mornings or evenings, and comfortable, sturdy hiking shoes are essential for varied terrain. Don't forget sun protection like a hat and sunscreen.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Cañaveral?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't always detailed, Cañaveral is a rural area, and you can generally find parking in or near the town center, or at designated starting points for more popular routes. For routes starting from the train station, parking facilities are usually available nearby.
tips: Cañaveral is a Spanish town and municipality, located in the province of Cáceres, in the autonomous community of Extremadura. Administratively, it is part of the judicial district of Cáceres and the community of Riberos del Tajo. It is located in the center of the province, north of the Tagus River, near a chain of mountain ranges that extend from the Monfragüe National Park.
The gothic bridge of San Benito crosses the Arroyo de las Juntas just one kilometer from the town of Cañaveral. It is a medieval construction dating from the fourteenth century.
